_comment: Baseline of ACCEPTED parity gaps, keyed by feed id. A 'gap' is a MobilityData ERROR/WARNING notice code that our validator does NOT flag on the same feed. The parity test exits non-zero only when it finds a gap NOT listed here for that feed (a regression / newly-surfaced canonical rule). To accept a new gap intentionally, add its code to the feed's array and commit. Regenerate the suggested contents by running `npm run test:validator-parity -- --write-baseline`. INFO-severity notices are never gating. Regenerated against MobilityData v8.0.1 on 2026-07-12; updated 2026-07-15 for the #50 quality-check port (fast_travel_between_consecutive_stops/far_stops, stop_too_far_from_shape, feed_expiration_date7_days/30_days, decreasing_shape_distance, decreasing_or_equal_stop_time_distance, route_long_name_contains_short_name, same_name_and_description_for_route, duplicate_route_name), which the two sample-gtfs-feed fixtures now catch alongside the canonical validator — route_long_name_contains_short_name in particular moved from an accepted gap to Both-catch. Every remaining gap below is a feed-hygiene rule (feed_info recommended fields, stops with no stop_time). The flex feeds have ZERO flex gaps: the canonical validator and our validator agree on all 5 injected flex errors.
